critical_issues: Incorrect documentation of alcohol consumption frequency in MER ('1 unit' per week) compared to customer's statement ('occasionally/few months ago').
Incorrect documentation in MER that customer knew exact lipid reading when they explicitly stated they didn't have it readily available.
The doctor missed asking about eye-related disorders (Health.l) and ear, nose, or throat disorders (Health.m) from the MER template.
Customer initially stated 'No' to the list of gastrointestinal disorders (Health.i), but later disclosed a hernia, indicating an incomplete initial response that was not probed further by the doctor.
recommendations: Ensure all listed health conditions are explicitly addressed, even if the customer gives a general 'no' to a list, by probing for specific conditions if necessary.
Verify and accurately document quantitative responses (e.g., alcohol units) to match customer's qualitative statements.
Ensure all questions from the MER template, including BMI and specific system-wise health conditions (e.g., eye, ENT), are asked during the telephonic verification.
Improve internal communication regarding physical test bookings to avoid customer confusion and cancellations.
Address the spam call issue reported by the customer to improve call connect rates and customer experience.
